title: Control-Plane Constraint and Index Parity â€” Filesystem to PGlite
context: During migration from filesystem JSON to PGlite, every constraint and index enforced by the filesystem storage model must have an equivalent in PGlite. Silent data integrity regressions occur when constraints exist in one backend but not the other.
decision:
Document the constraint parity between filesystem and PGlite for all entity types.

CAMPAIGN:
- Filesystem: File name uniqueness enforced by OS. Slug uniqueness NOT enforced (same slug can exist with different IDs).
- PGlite: uniqueIndex on slug. Primary key on id. Indices on status, horizon, maturity.
- PARITY: PGlite is STRICTER (slug uniqueness added). Migration tool must detect duplicate slugs and resolve before insert.
- Verification: SELECT slug, COUNT(*) FROM campaigns GROUP BY slug HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 â€” must return zero rows.

MISSION:
- Filesystem: File name uniqueness. No cross-file FK validation (campaignId can reference non-existent campaign).
- PGlite: FK on campaign_id REFERENCES campaigns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. uniqueIndex on slug. Indices on campaign_id, status, horizon.
- PARITY: PGlite is STRICTER (FK enforced, slug uniqueness). Migration must order campaigns before missions. Orphan missions (invalid campaignId) must be flagged.
- Verification: SELECT mission_id FROM missions WHERE campaign_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM campaigns) â€” must return zero rows.

LANE:
- Filesystem: File name uniqueness. No FK to mission (missionId can be invalid).
- PGlite: FK on mission_id REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. uniqueIndex on slug. Indices on mission_id, status, lease_holder.
- PARITY: PGlite is STRICTER.

TASK:
- Filesystem: File name uniqueness. No FK validation.
- PGlite: FK on lane_id REFERENCES lanes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. FK on mission_id REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. uniqueIndex on slug. Indices on lane_id, mission_id, status, priority, risk_class, assigned_to.
- PARITY: PGlite is STRICTER.

RECEIPT:
- Filesystem: No receipts stored (generated on-the-fly from operation results).
- PGlite: Indices on entity_type+entity_id, actor, previous_receipt_id (hash chain), timestamp.
- PARITY: PGlite ADDS receipt storage (not present in filesystem).

CHECKPOINT:
- Filesystem: Stored in docs/json/omp/checkpoints/ with entity type and ID in filename.
- PGlite: FK on receipt_id REFERENCES receipts(id). Indices on entity_type+entity_id, receipt_id, time_created.
- PARITY: EQUIVALENT.

RESEARCH PACKET:
- Filesystem: Stored in docs/json/omp/research/ with slug-based naming.
- PGlite: uniqueIndex on slug. Index on research_topic.
- PARITY: EQUIVALENT (slug uniqueness added).

INDEX PARITY SUMMARY:
- 8 unique indices (slug uniqueness per entity type)
- 19 query indices (status, campaign, mission, lane, horizon, maturity, priority, etc.)
- 6 foreign key constraints (campaignâ†’mission, missionâ†’lane, laneâ†’task, taskâ†’lane, taskâ†’mission, receiptâ†’checkpoint)
- 0 circular dependencies
- PGlite is STRICTER in 5 of 7 entity types (adds FK validation)
- No constraint exists in filesystem that is missing from PGlite
consequences: PGlite adds constraints that filesystem JSON storage could not enforce: slug uniqueness, foreign key integrity, and query-optimized indices. The migration tool must resolve constraint violations before insert (duplicate slugs, orphan references). Verification queries must run after migration to prove parity. The stricter PGlite constraints are an upgrade, not a regression.
